published_prior_art.cite: YinLiuGengLuo2020: optimal observed-data surrogate-paradox exclusion criteria; HsiaoTianParast2025: resilience measures for surrogate paradox under historical endpoint data

HuiWalter1980 supplies only two-test/two-population measurement algebra; FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2021 and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supply generic discrete bounding machinery, not the common-kernel surrogate bridge-drift formula

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses HungChen2026, 'The Proximal Surrogate Index,' a direct 2026 long-term-treatment/proxy comparator; it does not subsume the partial-ID noisy common-kernel boundary, but the field-tier claim needs an explicit comparison.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Hung--Chen (2026) to Related work and the Conjecture 2 why-new block, separating its exact proximal proxy identification from this proposal's finite noisy-surrogate partial-ID bridge-drift boundary.

Add a one-paragraph symbolic derivation of the Hui--Walter determinant identities leading to s_star, pi_star, and lambda_star so reviewers can see exactly which part is inherited and which part is new.

Sharpen Conjecture 2 by stating whether the union over bridge means is always an interval under the common q_star bridge set, or deliberately call it the sharp identified set rather than the sharp endpoint interval.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison for the missed proximal surrogate-index comparator and tighten the Conjecture 2 set/interval wording; no pivot needed.
